meaning: orchagent failed to program a route into the ASIC via SAI
likely_causes: ASIC FIB/TCAM table full -- check scale limits
SAI library bug or platform-specific constraint
syncd crashed or not running
Invalid next-hop object ID (neighbor not resolved)
next_steps: Check syncd status: docker exec syncd supervisorctl status syncd
Check ASIC utilization: show system-resources or crm show resources all
Review syncd log: docker exec syncd cat /var/log/syncd.log | tail -50
Check if next-hop neighbor exists: redis-cli -n 0 HGETALL 'NEIGH_TABLE:<interface>:<nexthop_ip>'

meaning: orchagent failed to program a neighbor (ARP/ND) entry into the ASIC
likely_causes: Neighbor table full in ASIC
Invalid interface/router interface object
syncd not responding
next_steps: Check neighbor table capacity: crm show resources all | grep neighbor
Verify ARP entry exists in kernel: ip neigh show <ip>
Check APPL_DB: redis-cli -n 0 HGETALL 'NEIGH_TABLE:<intf>:<ip>'

meaning: orchagent is falling behind processing APPL_DB notifications
likely_causes: CPU congestion -- too many routes being programmed simultaneously
syncd slow to respond (ASIC programming delay)
Memory pressure causing swap
next_steps: Check CPU: show processes cpu
Check memory: show system-memory
Check if bulk route update in progress (e.g., BGP convergence)

meaning: Port operational status changed -- link up or down event
likely_causes: Physical link change (cable, transceiver, peer port)
Admin shutdown/no-shutdown
Auto-negotiation failure
next_steps: Check interface status: show interfaces status
Check transceiver: show interfaces transceiver eeprom <interface>
Check for link flapping: show logging | grep <interface>

meaning: orchagent warm restart state change -- reconciliation in progress or complete
likely_causes: Warm restart was initiated via config warm_restart enable
swss container restarted with warm_restart enabled
next_steps: Check warm restart state: show warm_restart state
Wait for reconciliation to complete before making config changes

meaning: Critical Resource Monitoring detected resource usage above threshold
likely_causes: ASIC table approaching capacity (routes, neighbors, ACLs, next-hops)
Resource leak in orchagent
next_steps: Check all CRM resources: crm show resources all
Identify which resource is near capacity
Review if routes/neighbors can be reduced or TCAM profile adjusted

meaning: syncd failed to initialize the SAI API -- cannot communicate with ASIC
likely_causes: Vendor SAI library (libsai.so) missing or corrupt
ASIC hardware failure or not detected
Kernel driver for ASIC not loaded
Incompatible SAI library version
next_steps: Check if ASIC kernel module is loaded: lsmod | grep <vendor>
Check dmesg for hardware errors: dmesg | tail -50
Verify libsai.so exists: docker exec syncd ls -la /usr/lib/libsai*
Try cold reboot: sudo reboot

meaning: SAI API call returned an error status from the vendor ASIC SDK
likely_causes: SAI_STATUS_TABLE_FULL: ASIC table capacity reached
SAI_STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED: Feature not supported on this platform
SAI_STATUS_FAILURE: Generic vendor SDK error
SAI_STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER: Bad parameter from orchagent
next_steps: Decode the specific SAI status code from the log
Check ASIC resource usage: crm show resources all
Check platform capabilities with vendor documentation
If TABLE_FULL: reduce route/ACL/neighbor count or change TCAM profile

meaning: ASIC hardware notification received -- FDB learning event, port state change, or BFD session event
likely_causes: Normal ASIC operation -- hardware events propagated to software
FDB: MAC address learned/aged/moved
Port: Physical link state change detected by PHY
next_steps: Usually informational -- no action needed unless excessive
High rate of FDB notifications may indicate MAC flapping
High rate of port notifications may indicate link instability

meaning: bgpcfgd failed to render a Jinja2 template for FRR configuration
likely_causes: Invalid or missing fields in CONFIG_DB BGP tables
Custom template modification broke Jinja2 syntax
bgpcfgd code bug after SONiC upgrade
next_steps: Check CONFIG_DB BGP entries: redis-cli -n 4 KEYS 'BGP_*'
Check template: cat /usr/share/sonic/templates/bgpd.conf.j2
Check bgpcfgd logs: journalctl -u bgp | grep bgpcfgd | tail -30
Try regenerating: sonic-cfggen -d -t /usr/share/sonic/templates/bgpd.conf.j2

meaning: bgpcfgd's vtysh command to configure FRR was rejected
likely_causes: FRR (bgpd) rejected the configuration command
Syntax error in generated vtysh command
FRR is not running or crashed
next_steps: Check FRR status: docker exec bgp supervisorctl status
Try the vtysh command manually: docker exec bgp vtysh -c '<command>'
Check FRR log: docker exec bgp cat /var/log/frr/bgpd.log | tail -30

meaning: bgpcfgd detected a CONFIG_DB change and is syncing to FRR
likely_causes: Normal operation -- user made a config change via CLI/API
next_steps: Usually informational -- verify the change took effect in FRR: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config'
